<a id="section-87"></a>
# Section 87: Payment Method
**Original Source**: [https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_method.html](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_method.html)

---


# Payment MethodÂ

Search and return the payment methods matching the compatibility criteria.

The compatibility criteria are that payment methods must: be supported by at least one of
the providers; support the country of the partner if it exists; be primary payment methods
(not a brand). If provided, the optional keyword arguments further refine the criteria.

- **provider_ids** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)) â The list of providers by which the payment methods must be at
least partially supported to be considered compatible, as a list
of `payment.provider` ids.
- **partner_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The partner making the payment, as a `res.partner` id.
- **currency_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The payment currency, if known beforehand, as a `res.currency` id.
- **force_tokenization** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether only payment methods supporting tokenization can be
matched.
- **is_express_checkout** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the payment is made through express checkout.
- **report** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The report in which each providerâs availability status and reason must
be logged.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Optional data. This parameter is not used here.

The compatible payment methods.

payment.method

Get the payment method corresponding to the given provider-specific code.

If a mapping is given, the search uses the generic payment method code that corresponds to
the given provider-specific code.

- **code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The provider-specific code of the payment method to get.
- **mapping** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â A non-exhaustive mapping of generic payment method codes to
provider-specific codes.

The corresponding payment method, if any.

payment.method


---


<a id="section-88"></a>
# Section 88: Payment Provider
**Original Source**: [https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_provider.html](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_provider.html)

---


# Payment ProviderÂ

Set the basic HTTP Auth of the request

This method serves as a hook to allow providers to build the requestâs basic HTTP Auth.

**kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Provider-specific data.

The basic HTTP Auth, if any.

[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)

Build the headers of the request.

This method serves as a hook to allow providers to build the request headers.

- **method** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The HTTP method of the request.
- **endpoint** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The endpoint of the API to reach with the request.
- **payload** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payload of the request.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Provider-specific data.

The request headers.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Build the URL of the request.

This method serves as a hook to allow providers to build the request URL.

- **endpoint** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The endpoint of the API to reach with the request.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Provider-specific data.

The request URL.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Compute the feature support fields based on the provider.

Feature support fields are used to specify which additional features are supported by a
given provider. These fields are as follows:

- `support_express_checkout`: Whether the âexpress checkoutâ feature is supported. `False`
by default.
- `support_manual_capture`: Whether the âmanual captureâ feature is supported. `False` by
default.
- `support_refund`: Which type of the ârefundsâ feature is supported: `None`,
`'full_only'`, or `'partial'`. `None` by default.
- `support_tokenization`: Whether the âtokenization featureâ is supported. `False` by
default.

For a provider to specify that it supports additional features, it must override this method
and set the related feature support fields to the desired value on the appropriate
`payment.provider` records.

None

Return the code of the provider.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

The code of the provider.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Search and return the providers matching the compatibility criteria.

The compatibility criteria are that providers must: not be disabled; be in the company that
is provided; support the country of the partner if it exists; be compatible with the
currency if provided. If provided, the optional keyword arguments further refine the
criteria.

- **company_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The company to which providers must belong, as a `res.company` id.
- **partner_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The partner making the payment, as a `res.partner` id.
- **amount** ([*float*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)) â The amount to pay. `0` for validation transactions.
- **currency_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The payment currency, if known beforehand, as a `res.currency` id.
- **force_tokenization** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether only providers allowing tokenization can be matched.
- **is_express_checkout** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the payment is made through express checkout.
- **is_validation** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the operation is a validation.
- **report** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The report in which each providerâs availability status and reason must
be logged.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Optional data. This parameter is not used here.

The compatible providers.

payment.provider

Return the default payment methods for this provider.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

The default payment method codes.

[set](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set)

Return the view of the template used to render the redirect form.

For a provider to return a different view depending on whether the operation is a
validation, it must override this method and return the appropriate view.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

**is_validation** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the operation is a validation.

The view of the redirect form template.

record of `ir.ui.view`

Return the payment provider domain.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider to search for.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Additional keyword arguments.

The domain to search for the provider.

[list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)[[tuple](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)]

Return the values to update a provider with when its module is uninstalled.

For a module to specify additional removal values, it must override this method and complete
the generic values with its specific values.

The removal values to update the removed provider with.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Return the supported currencies for the payment provider.

By default, all currencies are considered supported, including the inactive ones. For a
provider to filter out specific currencies, it must override this method and return the
subset of supported currencies.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

The supported currencies.

res.currency

Return the amount to use for validation operations.

For a provider to support tokenization, it must override this method and return the
validation amount. If it is `0`, it is not necessary to create the override.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

The validation amount.

[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)

Return the currency to use for validation operations.

The validation currency must be supported by both the provider and the payment method. If
the payment method is not passed, only the providerâs supported currencies are considered.
If no suitable currency is found, the providerâs companyâs currency is returned instead.

For a provider to support tokenization and specify a different validation currency, it must
override this method and return the appropriate validation currency.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

The validation currency.

recordset of `res.currency`

Return whether tokenizing the transaction is required given its context.

For a module to make the tokenization required based on the payment context, it must
override this method and return whether it is required.

**kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payment context. This parameter is not used here.

Whether tokenizing the transaction is required.

[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)

Retrieve the JSON-formatted content of the response.

This method serves as a hook to allow providers to parse the response content.

- **response** (*requests.Response*) â The response to parse.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Provider-specific data.

The response content.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Retrieve the error message from the response.

This method serves as a hook to allow providers to parse the responseâs error message.

**response** (*requests.Response*) â The response to parse.

The error message.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Send a request to the API.

Whenever possible, calls to this method should be wrapped in a try-except block to prevent
the `ValidationError` that is raised when the request fails from bubbling up. Exceptions to
this rule include calls from a controller that must return the error message to the client.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

- **method** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The HTTP method of the request.
- **endpoint** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The endpoint of the API to reach with the request.
- **params** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The query string parameters of the request.
- **data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)*|*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The body of the request.
- **json** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The JSON-formatted body of the request.
- **reference** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The reference of the transaction, if any.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Provider-specific data forwarded to the specialized helper methods.

The formatted content of the response.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)|[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

[**ValidationError**](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/backend/orm.html#odoo.exceptions.ValidationError) â If an HTTP error occurs.

Return whether the inline payment form should be instantiated.

For a provider to handle both direct payments and payments with redirection, it must
override this method and return whether the inline payment form should be instantiated (i.e.
if the payment should be direct) based on the operation (online payment or validation).

**is_validation** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the operation is a validation.

Whether the inline form should be instantiated.

[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)

<a id="section-89"></a>
# Section 89: Payment Token
**Original Source**: [https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_token.html](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_token.html)

---


# Payment TokenÂ

Return the available tokens linked to the given providers and partner.

For a module to retrieve the available tokens, it must override this method and add
information in the kwargs to define the context of the request.

- **providers_ids** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)) â The ids of the providers available for the transaction.
- **partner_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The id of the partner.
- **is_validation** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the transaction is a validation operation.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Locally unused keywords arguments.

The available tokens.

payment.token

Build a token name of the desired maximum length with the format `â¢â¢â¢â¢ 1234`.

The payment details are padded on the left with up to four padding characters. The padding
is only added if there is enough room for it. If not, it is either reduced or not added at
all. If there is not enough room for the payment details either, they are trimmed from the
left.

For a module to customize the display name of a token, it must override this method and
return the customized display name.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

- **args** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)) â The arguments passed by QWeb when calling this method.
- **max_length** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â The desired maximum length of the token name. The default is `34` to
fit the largest IBANs.
- **should_pad** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â Whether the token should be padded.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Optional data used in overrides of this method.

The padded token name.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Complete the values of the `create` method with provider-specific values.

For a provider to add its own create values, it must overwrite this method and return a
dict of values. Provider-specific values take precedence over those of the dict of generic
create values.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider managing the token.
- **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The original create values.

The dict of provider-specific create values.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Handle the archiving of tokens.

For a module to perform additional operations when a token is archived, it must override
this method.

None

Return a list of information about records linked to the current token.

For a module to implement payments and link documents to a token, it must override this
method and add information about linked document records to the returned list.

The information must be structured as a dict with the following keys:

- `description`: The description of the recordâs model (e.g. âSubscriptionâ).
- `id`: The id of the record.
- `name`: The name of the record.
- `url`: The url to access the record.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

The list of information about the linked document records.

[list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)


---


<a id="section-90"></a>
# Section 90: Payment Transaction
**Original Source**: [https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_transaction.html](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_transaction.html)

---


# Payment TransactionÂ

Update the transaction based on the payment data received from the provider.

The updates typically include the paymentâs state, the provider reference, and the selected
payment method.

This method should not be called directly; payment data should go through [`_process()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_transaction.html#odoo.addons.payment.models.payment_transaction.PaymentTransaction._process).

This method must be overridden by providers to update the transaction based on the payment
data.

Note: `self.ensure_one()` from [`_process()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_transaction.html#odoo.addons.payment.models.payment_transaction.PaymentTransaction._process)

**payment_data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payment data sent by the provider.

None

Compute a unique reference for the transaction.

The reference corresponds to the prefix if no other transaction with that prefix already
exists. Otherwise, it follows the pattern `{computed_prefix}{separator}{sequence_number}`
where:

- `{computed_prefix}` is:

  - The provided custom prefix, if any.
  - The computation result of [`_compute_reference_prefix()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_transaction.html#odoo.addons.payment.models.payment_transaction.PaymentTransaction._compute_reference_prefix) if the custom prefix is not
filled, but the kwargs are.
  - `'tx-{datetime}'` if neither the custom prefix nor the kwargs are filled.
- `{separator}` is the string that separates the prefix from the sequence number.
- `{sequence_number}` is the next integer in the sequence of references sharing the same
prefix. The sequence starts with `1` if there is only one matching reference.

Example

- Given the custom prefix `'example'` which has no match with an existing reference, the
full reference will be `'example'`.
- Given the custom prefix `'example'` which matches the existing reference `'example'`,
and the custom separator `'-'`, the full reference will be `'example-1'`.
- Given the kwargs `{'invoice_ids': [1, 2]}`, the custom separator `'-'` and no custom
prefix, the full reference will be `'INV1-INV2'` (or similar) if no existing reference
has the same prefix, or `'INV1-INV2-n'` if `n` existing references have the same
prefix.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider handling the transaction.
- **prefix** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The custom prefix used to compute the full reference.
- **separator** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The custom separator used to separate the prefix from the suffix.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Optional values passed to [`_compute_reference_prefix()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_transaction.html#odoo.addons.payment.models.payment_transaction.PaymentTransaction._compute_reference_prefix) if no custom
prefix is provided.

The unique reference for the transaction.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Compute the reference prefix from the transaction values.

Note: This method should be called in sudo mode to give access to the documents (invoices,
sales orders) referenced in the transaction values.

- **separator** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The custom separator used to separate parts of the computed
reference prefix.
- **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The transaction values used to compute the reference prefix.

The computed reference prefix.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Extract the amount, currency and rounding precision from the payment data.

This method must be overridden by providers to parse the amount data from the payment data.
If the provider returns `None`, the amount validation is skipped.

**payment_data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payment data sent by the provider.

The amount data, in the {amount: float, currency_code: str, precision_digits: int}
format.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)|[None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)

Extract the transaction reference from the payment data.

This method must be overridden by providers to extract the reference from the payment data.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider handling the transaction.
- **payment_data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payment data sent by the provider.

The transaction reference.

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

Extract the create values of a token from the payment data.

Providers can override this to supply their own token data based on the payment data.

Note: self.ensure_one() from :meth: `_tokenize`

**payment_data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Data sent by the provider.

Data to create a payment token.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Complete the values of the `create` method with provider-specific values.

For a provider to add its own create values, it must overwrite this method and return a dict
of values. Provider-specific values take precedence over those of the dict of generic create
values.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider that handled the transaction.
- **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The original create values.

The dict of provider-specific create values.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Return a dict of provider-specific values used to process the transaction.

For a provider to add its own processing values, it must overwrite this method and return a
dict of provider-specific values based on the generic values returned by this method.
Provider-specific values take precedence over those of the dict of generic processing
values.

**processing_values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The generic processing values of the transaction.

The dict of provider-specific processing values.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Return a dict of provider-specific values used to render the redirect form.

For a provider to add its own rendering values, it must overwrite this method and return a
dict of provider-specific values based on the processing values (provider-specific
processing values included).

**processing_values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The processing values of the transaction.

The dict of provider-specific rendering values.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Process the payment data received from the provider and update the transaction.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider handling the transaction.
- **payment_data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payment data sent by the provider.

The updated transaction.

payment.transaction

Search the transaction based on the payment data.

- **provider_code** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The code of the provider handling the transaction.
- **payment_data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The payment data sent by the provider.

The transaction, if found.

payment.transaction

Send a request to the API.

This method serves as a helper to:

1. Pass the transaction reference to the providerâs
[`_send_api_request()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/standard_modules/payment/payment_provider.html#odoo.addons.payment.models.payment_provider.PaymentProvider._send_api_request)
method.
2. Set the transactionâs state to `error` if the request fails, with the exceptionâs message
as the `state_message`.

Note: `self.ensure_one()`

- **method** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The HTTP method of the request.
- **endpoint** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The endpoint of the API to reach with the request.
- **params** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The query string parameters of the request.
- **data** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)*|*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The body of the request.
- **json** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â The JSON-formatted body of the request.
- **kwargs** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â Provider-specific data forwarded to the specialized helper methods.

The formatted content of the response.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)|[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)

[**ValidationError**](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/backend/orm.html#odoo.exceptions.ValidationError) â If an HTTP error occurs.

Request the provider handling the transaction to send a capture request.

For a provider to support authorization, it must override this method and send an API
request to capture the payment.

Note: `self.ensure_one()` from `_capture()`

None

Request the provider handling the transaction to send a token payment request.

This method is exclusively used to make payments by token, which correspond to both the
`online_token` and the `offline` transactionâs `operation` field.

For a provider to support tokenization, it must override this method and send an API request
to make a payment.

Note: `self.ensure_one()` from `_charge_with_token()`

None

Request the provider handling the transaction to send a refund request.

For a provider to support refunds, it must override this method and send an API request to
make a refund.

Note: `self.ensure_one()` from `_refund()`

None

Request the provider handling the transaction to send a void request.

For a provider to support authorization, it must override this method and send an API
request to void the payment.

Note: `self.ensure_one()` from `_void()`

None

Update the transactionsâ state to `authorized`.

- **state_message** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The reason for setting the transactions in the state `authorized`.
- **extra_allowed_states** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*]*) â The extra states that should be considered allowed
target states for the source state âauthorizedâ.

The updated transactions.

recordset of `payment.transaction`

Update the transactionsâ state to `cancel`.

- **state_message** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The reason for setting the transactions in the state `cancel`.
- **extra_allowed_states** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*]*) â The extra states that should be considered allowed
target states for the source state âcanceledâ.

The updated transactions.

recordset of `payment.transaction`

Update the transactionsâ state to `done`.

- **state_message** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The reason for setting the transactions in the state `done`.
- **extra_allowed_states** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*]*) â The extra states that should be considered allowed
target states for the source state âdoneâ.

The updated transactions.

recordset of `payment.transaction`

Update the transactionsâ state to `error`.

- **state_message** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The reason for setting the transactions in the state `error`.
- **extra_allowed_states** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*]*) â The extra states that should be considered allowed
target states for the source state âerrorâ.

The updated transactions.

recordset of `payment.transaction`

Update the transactionsâ state to `pending`.

- **state_message** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â The reason for setting the transactions in the state `pending`.
- **extra_allowed_states** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*[*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*]*) â The extra states that should be considered allowed
target states for the source state âpendingâ.

The updated transactions.

<a id="section-92"></a>
# Section 92: Upgrade Scripts
**Original Source**: [https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_scripts.html](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_scripts.html)

---


# Upgrade scriptsÂ

An upgrade script is a Python file containing a function called [`migrate()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_scripts.html#migrate), which the upgrade
process invokes during the update of a module.

- **cr** (`Cursor`) â current database cursor
- **version** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â installed version of the module

Typically, this function executes one or multiple SQL queries and can also access Odooâs ORM, as
well as the [Upgrade utils](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html).


## Writing upgrade scriptsÂ

Upgrade scripts follow a specific tree structure with a naming convention which determines when they
are executed.

The structure of an upgrade script path is `$module/migrations/$version/pre,post,end-*.py`,
where `$module` is the module for which the script will run, `$version` is the full version of the
module (including Odooâs major version and the moduleâs minor version) and `{pre|post|end}-*.py` is
the file that needs to be executed. The fileâs name will determine the [phase](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_scripts.html#upgrade-scripts-phases) and order in which it is executed for that module and version.

Note

From Odoo 13 the top-level directory for the upgrade scripts can also be named `upgrades`. This
naming is preferred since it has the correct meaning: *migrate* can be confused with *moving out
of Odoo*. Thus `$module/upgrades/$version/` is also valid.

Note

Upgrade scripts are only executed when the module is being updated. Therefore, the
moduleâs minor version set in the `$version` directory needs to be higher than the moduleâs
installed version and equal or lower to the updated version of the module.

Example

Directory structure of an upgrade script for a custom module named `awesome_partner` upgraded
to version `2.0` on Odoo 17.

```text
awesome_partner/
|-- migrations/
|   |-- 17.0.2.0/
|   |   |-- pre-exclamation.py

```

Two upgrade scripts examples with the content of the `pre-exclamation.py`, file adding
â!â at the end of partnersâ names:

```python
import logging

_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def migrate(cr, version):
    cr.execute("UPDATE res_partner SET name = name || '!'")
    _logger.info("Updated %s partners", cr.rowcount)

```

```python
import logging
from odoo.upgrade import util

_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def migrate(cr, version):
    env = util.env(cr)

    partners = env["res.partner"].search([])
    for partner in partners:
        partner.name += "!"

    _logger.info("Updated %s partners", len(partners))

```

Note that in the second example, the script takes advantage of the [Upgrade utils](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html) to
access the ORM. Check the documentation to find out more about this library.


## Phases of upgrade scriptsÂ

The upgrade process consists of three phases for each version of each module:

1. The pre-phase, before the module is loaded.
2. The post-phase, after the module and its dependencies are loaded and updated.
3. The end-phase, after all modules have been loaded and updated for that version.

Upgrade scripts are grouped according to the first part of their filenames into the corresponding
phase. Within each phase, the files are executed according to their lexical order.

Execution order of example scripts for one module in one version

1. `pre-10-do_something.py`
2. `pre-20-something_else.py`
3. `post-do_something.py`
4. `post-something.py`
5. `end-01-migrate.py`
6. `end-migrate.py`


---


<a id="section-93"></a>
# Section 93: Upgrade Utils
**Original Source**: [https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html)

---


# Upgrade utilsÂ

[Upgrade utils](https://github.com/odoo/upgrade-util/) is a library that contains helper functions
to facilitate the writing of upgrade scripts. This library, used by Odoo for the upgrade scripts of
standard modules, provides reliability and helps speed up the upgrade process:

- The helper functions help make sure the data is consistent in the database.
- It takes care of indirect references of the updated records.
- Allows calling functions and avoid writing code, saving time and reducing development risks.
- Helpers allow to focus on what is important for the upgrade and not think of details.


## InstallationÂ

Clone the [Upgrade utils repository](https://github.com/odoo/upgrade-util/) locally and start
`odoo` with the `src` directory prepended to the `--upgrade-path` option.

```console
$ ./odoo-bin --upgrade-path=/path/to/upgrade-util/src,/path/to/other/upgrade/script/directory [...]

```

On platforms where you do not manage Odoo yourself, you can install this library via `pip`:

```console
$ python3 -m pip install git+https://github.com/odoo/upgrade-util@master

```

On [Odoo.sh](https://www.odoo.sh/) it is recommended to add it to the `requirements.txt` of
the custom repository. For this, add the following line inside the file:

```default
odoo_upgrade @ git+https://github.com/odoo/upgrade-util@master

```


## Using upgrade utilsÂ

Once installed, the following packages are available for the upgrade scripts:

- `odoo.upgrade.util`: the helper itself.
- [`odoo.upgrade.testing`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#module-odoo.upgrade.testing): base TestCase classes.

To use it in upgrade scripts, simply import it:

```python
from odoo.upgrade import util


def migrate(cr, version):
   # Rest of the script

```

Now, the helper functions are available to be called through `util`.


## Util functionsÂ

Upgrade utils provides many useful functions to ease the upgrade process. Here, we describe some
of the most useful ones. Refer to the [util folder](https://github.com/odoo/upgrade-util/tree/master/src/util) for the comprehensive declaration of
helper functions.

Note

The `cr` parameter in util functions always refers to the database cursor. Pass the one
received as a parameter in [`migrate()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_scripts.html#migrate). Not all functions need this parameter.


### ModulesÂ

Utility functions for module-level operations.

In most cases module operations (rename, merge, remove, â¦) should be performed in a
`base` script. The reason is that once the `base` module is upgraded, all the information
regarding modules should be already set in the DB for the upgrade process to function
correctly. The command line option `--pre-upgrade-scripts` (available from Odoo 16) allows
to run upgrade scripts *before* loading base. This is the recommended way to perform
module operations after a major upgrade.

Return whether *all* given modules are installed.

**modules** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â names of the modules to check

[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)

Note

In the context of upgrades a module is considered as installed if it is marked for
upgrade, or for installation; even if they are not yet fully installed.

Return whether a module is installed.

**module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module to check

[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)

See [`modules_installed()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.modules.modules_installed).

Uninstall and remove all records owned by a module.

**module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module to uninstall

Uninstall a theme module and remove it from websites.

- **theme** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the theme module to uninstall
- **base_theme** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â if not `None`, unload first this base theme

Warning

This function can only be used in `post-` scripts of `website` module as it relies
on the ORM.

See [`remove_theme()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.modules.remove_theme) and [`uninstall_module()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.modules.uninstall_module).

Completely remove a module.

This operation is equivalent to uninstall and removal of *all* references to
the module - no trace of it is left in the database.

**module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module to remove

Warning

Since this function removes *all* data associated to the module. Ensure to
reassign records before calling this function.

Uninstall a theme module.

Warning

This function can only be used in `post-` scripts.

See [`remove_module()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.modules.remove_module) and [`uninstall_theme()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.modules.uninstall_theme).

Rename a module and all references to it.

- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â current name of the module to rename
- **new** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â new name of the module to rename

Merge a module into another.

This function moves all references and records from the source module to the
destination module.

Warning

This functions does not remove any record, but it removes xml_ids from the source
module with a conflicting name in the destination module.

- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module to be merged
- **into** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module to merge into
- **update_dependers** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether the dependencies of modules that depend on `old`
are updated

Force the ORM to install a module.

- **module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module to install
- **if_installed** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â only force the install when these modules are
already installed

the *new* state of the module

Force the execution of upgrade scripts for a module that is being installed.

Standard Odoo doesnât run upgrade scripts when a module is being installed. This makes
sense because, technically speaking, the module is not being upgraded. Still, it
happens that a (new) module needs to perform some operations for it to be correctly
installed, like grabbing data from another module. This is common when a module is
functionally *split* into several ones.

- **module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module for which to force the execution of upgrade
scripts
- **init** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to set the module to be in *init* mode

Being in init mode has the side effect of not respecting noupdate flags, in XML file
nor in `ir_model_data`.

Move a model from one module to another.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the model to move
- **from_module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module where the model is originally defined
- **to_module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the destination module where the model is to be moved
- **move_data** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to also update `ir_model_data` for records of the model
- **keep** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â list of XML ids to keep - not move

This function can be called for moving overrides of a model to another module. As it
cannot distinguish between source model or inherited model, it raises an exception if
the destination module isnât installed.


### ModelsÂ

Utility functions for modifying models.

Model operations are best done in `pre-` script of the involved modules.

Remove a model and its references from the database.

Some required indirect references to the model are replaced by the *unknown model* -
an empty model that serves as placeholder for gone models.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the model to remove
- **drop_table** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to drop the table of this model
- **ignore_m2m** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â list of m2m tables to ignore - not removed, use
`"*"` to ignore (keep) all m2m tables

Rename a model.

Updates all references to the model name in the DB.

If table rename is requested, from saas~18.1+, m2m table are updated too, unless
ignored. In older versions, m2m tables are skipped unless an empty list is passed.

- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â current model name
- **new** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â new model name
- **rename_table** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to also rename the table of the model
- **ignored_m2ms** â m2m tables to skip. Defaults to `"ALL_BEFORE_18_1"`, which skips
all in Odoo 18 or below, none in saa~18.1+. For all versions, if
the value is not the default, skip only the specified m2m tables.

Merge a model into another one.

This function moves all the references from `source` model into `target` model and
*removes* `source` model and its references. By default, only the fields with the
same name in both models are moved from source to target, optionally a mapping with
differently named fields can be provided.

Warning

This function does not move the records from `source` model to `target` model.

- **source** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the model to be merged
- **target** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the destination model to merge into
- **drop_table** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to drop the table of the source model
- **fields_mapping** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â mapping of field names from source to target
model, when `None` only fields with same name are
moved
- **ignore_m2m** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â list of m2m tables ignored to remove from source model.

Remove `inherit` from `model`.

This function removes all fields inherited via `inherit` from a `model` and all
its descendant models. All fields from the inherit, including those from its parent
models are also removed, unless `with_inherit_parents` mode is unset. In such case
only fields from `inherit` are removed, excluding fields in its parents. Fields
listed in `keep` are never removed. If some descendants from `model` are listed in
`skip_inherit` they will keep the fields from `inherit`.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the model from which to remove the inherit
- **inherit** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the inherited model (or mixin) to remove
- **keep** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â tuple with field names to keep
- **skip_inherit** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â list of descendant models of `model` to not process
- **with_inherit_parents** (*boolean*) â if unset, remove fields coming from `inherit`
only, keeping all fields from its parents
- **skip_update_references** ([*tuple*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or **"*"*) â field names whose references should not be
updated upon deletion. Use `"*"` for all fields.


### FieldsÂ

Utility functions for modifying model fields.

Field operations are best done in `pre-` script of the involved modules. In some cases a
preliminary operation could be done in pre and finished in post. A common example is to
remove a field in pre- keeping its column, later used in post when the column is finally
dropped.

Remove a field and its references from the database.

This function also removes the field from inheriting models, unless exceptions are
specified in `skip_inherit`. When the field is stored we can choose to not drop the
column.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the field to remove
- **fieldname** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the field to remove
- **cascade** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether the field column(s) are removed in `CASCADE` mode
- **drop_column** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether the fieldâs column is dropped
- **skip_inherit** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â list of inheriting models to skip the removal
of the field, use `"*"` to skip all
- **keep_as_attachments** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â for binary fields, whether the data should be kept
as attachments
- **update_references** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to update all references.
If `False`, donât update dashboard, filters, export paths,
domains, and relations for related fields.

Convert field to non-stored.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the field to convert
- **fieldname** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the field to convert
- **selectable** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether the field is still selectable, if True custom `ir.filters` are not updated

Move a field from one module to another.

This functions updates all references to a specific field, switching from the source
module to the destination module. It avoid data removal after the registry is fully
loaded. The field in inheriting models are also moved unless skipped.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the owner model of the field to move
- **fieldname** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the field to move
- **old_module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â source module name from which the field is moved
- **new_module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â target module name into which the field is moved
- **skip_inherit** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â list of inheriting models for which the field is
not to be moved, use `"*"` to skip all

Rename a field and its references from `old` to `new` on the given `model`.

The field is updated in all inheriting models, except for models specified in
`skip_inherit`.

This functions also updates references, indirect or direct ones, including filters,
server actions, related fields, emails, dashboards, domains, and many more. See
[`update_field_usage()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.fields.update_field_usage)

For the update of domains a special adapter function can be used. The default adapter
just replaces `old` by `new` in each domain leaf. Refer to
[`adapt_domains()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.domains.adapt_domains) for information about domain adapters.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the field to rename
- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â current name of the field to rename
- **new** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â new name of the field to rename
- **update_references** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to update all references
- **domain_adapter** (*function*) â adapter to use for domains, see
[`adapt_domains()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.domains.adapt_domains)
- **skip_inherit** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â models to skip when renaming the field in
inheriting models, use `"*"` to skip all

Rename a boolean field and invert its value.

Convert a Many2one field to a Many2many.

It creates the relation table and fill it.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the field to convert
- **field** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â current name of the field to convert
- **new_name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â new name of the field to convert.
If not provide, itâs `field` with a trailing âsâ.
- **m2m_table** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the relation table. Automatically generated if not provided.
- **col1** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the column referencing `model`.
- **col2** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the column referencing the target of `field`.

Replace references of values of a selection field.

This function replaces all references to selection values according to a mapping.
Domains are also updated.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the selection field to update
- **field** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the selection field to update
- **mapping** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â selection values to update, key values are replaced by
their corresponding values in the mapping
- **skip_inherit** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â list of inheriting models to skip in the update
of the selection values, use `"*"` to skip all

Replace all references to the field `old` by `new` in different places.

- ir_filters
- ir_exports_line
- ir_act_server
- mail_alias
- ir_ui_view_custom (dashboard)
- sign_item_type
- sign_item
- domains (using `domain_adapter`)
- related fields

This function can be used to replace the usage of a field by another. Domains are
updated using the `domain_adapter`. By default the domain adapter just replaces `old`
by `new` in domain leaves. See [`adapt_domains()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.domains.adapt_domains) for
more information about domain adapters.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the field
- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â source name of the field to replace
- **new** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â target name of the field to set
- **domain_adapter** (*function*) â adapter to use for domains, see
[`adapt_domains()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.domains.adapt_domains)
- **skip_inherit** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â models to skip when renaming the field in
inheriting models, use `"*"` to skip all

Post a chatter message on each record to preserve a column value before it is dropped.

This is useful when a field is being removed during a migration but clients may need to
recover its values. The value is posted as a `mail_message` on the recordâs chatter.

If the `mail` module is not installed, the function silently does nothing.

Example

```python
# Simple varchar column
util.dump_field_to_chatter(
    cr, "crm_lead", "mobile", label="Previous Mobile", where="phone != mobile"
)

# Drop team_id, save the team name
util.dump_field_to_chatter(
    cr, "res_partner", "team_id", label="Former Sales Team", fk_display="name"
)

# Dump from hr_version onto hr_employee's chatter
util.dump_field_to_chatter(
    cr, "hr_employee", "current_version_id", label="SSN", fk_display="ssnid"
)

# Raw HTML body (no label prefix)
util.dump_field_to_chatter(
    cr, "hr_employee", "notes", html_escape=False,
    message_type="comment", subtype_xmlid="mail.mt_note"
)

```

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model of the field to dump
- **field** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â field whose value to preserve
- **label** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â message format. Any valid PG `format` string with a single `%s`
is allowed. If `%s` is missing, `: %s` is appended automatically.
Default `"%s"` produces the raw value with no prefix.
- **res_id** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â column that identifies the id of the record to put in the chatter.
- **res_model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â model of the record to post in the chatter, by default
it is inferred from `res_id` target.
- **fk_display** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â when `column` is a foreign key, the column from the
FK target table to use as display value (e.g.
`"name"`). The FK target table is auto-detected. When
omitted, only the id is shown. The id is always
appended in parentheses.
- **where** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â extra SQL `WHERE` condition. By default, only rows where the
column is not `NULL` (and not empty for text types) are included.
- **html_escape** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to HTML-escape the value (default `True`).
- **message_type** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â `mail_message.message_type` (default `"notification"`).
- **subtype_xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â optional XML ID for the message subtype (e.g.
`"mail.mt_note"`).


### RecordsÂ

Utility functions for record-level operations.

Remove a view and all its descendants.

This function recursively deletes the given view and its inherited views, as long as
they are part of a module. It will fail as soon as a custom view exists anywhere in
the hierarchy. It also removes multi-website COWed views.

- **xml_id** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â optional, the xml_id of the view to remove
- **view_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â optional, the ID of the view to remove
- **silent** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to show in the logs disabled custom views
- **key** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â key used to detect multi-website COWed views, if `None` then
set to `xml_id` if provided, otherwise set to the xml_id
referencing the view with ID `view_id` if any

Warning

Either `xml_id` or `view_id` must be set. Specifying both will raise an error.

Context manager to edit a viewâs arch.

This function returns a context manager that may yield a parsed arch of a view as an
[etree Element](https://lxml.de/tutorial.html#the-element-class). Any changes done
in the returned object will be written back to the database upon exit of the context
manager, updating also the translated versions of the arch. Since the function may not
yield, use [`skippable_cm()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.misc.skippable_cm) to avoid errors.

```python
with util.skippable_cm(), util.edit_view(cr, "xml.id") as arch:
    arch.attrib["string"] = "My Form"

```

To select the target view to edit use either `xmlid` or `view_id`, not both.

When the view is identified by `view_id`, the arch is always yielded if the view
exists, with disregard to any `noupdate` flag it may have associated. When `xmlid` is
set, if the view `noupdate` flag is `False` then the arch will not be yielded *unless*
`skip_if_not_noupdate` is set to `False`. If `noupdate` is `True`, the view will be
yielded for edit.

If the `active` argument is `True` or `False`, the `active` flag of the view will be set
accordingly.

Note

If `active` is âautoâ (default value), the view will be activated if selected
via `xmlid` and left untouched if selected via `view_id`.

- **xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â optional, xml_id of the view edit
- **view_id** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â optional, ID of the view to edit
- **skip_if_not_noupdate** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to force the edit of views requested via
`xmlid` parameter even if they are flagged as
`noupdate=True`, ignored if `view_id` is set
- **active** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)* or **"auto"*) â active flag value to set. Unchanged when `None`.

a context manager that yields the parsed arch, upon exit the context manager
writes back the changes.

Remove a record and its references corresponding to the given [xml_id](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/glossary.html#term-external-identifier).

**name** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â record xml_id, under the format `module.name`

Return whether a record was changed.

This function checks if a record was changed before the current upgrade start time.
See `upgrade-util/src/base/0.0.0/pre-00-upgrade-start.py`

This utility will return a false positive on xmlids of records that match the
following conditions:

- Have been updated in an upgrade preceding the current one
- Have not been updated in the current upgrade

If the `xmlid` doesnât exist in the DB this function returns `None`.

- **xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â `xmlid` of the record to check
- **interval** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â SQL interval, a record is considered as changed if
`write_date > create_date + interval`

[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) or [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)

Run `callback` if a record is unchanged.

This function will run a `callback` when the referred record is unchanged. The `xmlid`
and any extra parameter, but not `interval`, will be passed to the `callback`. In case
the record was changed it will be marked as `noupdate=True`. See also
[`is_changed()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.records.is_changed) and
[`force_noupdate()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.records.force_noupdate).

This function is useful to take an action *only* when a record hasnât been updated, a
common example is to force an update from XML even if the record was `noupdate=True`

```python
util.if_unchanged(cr, "mymodule.myrecord", util.update_record_from_xml)

```

- **xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â xml_id of the record to check
- **callback** (*function*) â callback to execute in case the record was *not* changed, all
extra parameters to this function are passed to the callback
- **interval** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â interval after `create_date` on which a record is considered as
_changed_, see `is_changed()`

Rename an [external identifier](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/glossary.html#term-external-identifier) (`xml_id`) of a record.

A rename cannot happen when the target name already exists on the database. In such
cases there are two options to control how this function behaves:

- `fail`: raise an exception and prevent renaming
- `merge`: rename the external identifier, remove the old one, and *replace*
references. See [`replace_record_references_batch()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.records.replace_record_references_batch)

Note

This function does not remove records, it only updates xml_ids.

- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â current xml_id of the record, under the format `module.name`
- **new** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â new xml_id of the record, under the format `module.name`
- **noupdate** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)* or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â value to set on the `noupdate` flag of the xml_id,
ignored if `None`
- **on_collision** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â how to proceed if the xml_id already exists, the options are
`merge` or `fail` (default)

the ID of the record referenced by the *new* xml_id, `None` when the record
doesnât exist

[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) or [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)

Return the id corresponding to the given [xml_id](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/glossary.html#term-external-identifier).

**xml_id** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â record xml_id, under the format `module.name`

ID of the referenced record, `None` if not found.

[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) or [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)

Return a mapping of xmlid -> res_id for the given list of xmlids.

- **xmlids** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)) â list of fully qualified xml_ids, each under the format `module.name`
- **strict** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â if `True`, only include xmlids that exist in the database.

dict mapping each xmlid to its res_id; missing xmlids map to `None` unless
`strict=True`, in which case they are omitted from the result.

[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)

Update the `noupdate` flag of a record.

- **xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â record xml_id, under the format `module.name`
- **noupdate** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â value to set on the `noupdate` flag
- **warn** â whether to output a warning in the logs when the flag was switched from
`True` to `False`

Ensure an xml_id references a record with specific values.

This function ensures the record pointed by an xml_id matches the values for the
fields specified in the `values` parameter. When the xmlid exist but it points to a
record that doesnât match the values, the xmlid is updated to point to a record that
matches the values if one is found. If the xmlid doesnât exist, it is created with the
found record. When no matching record is found, nothing is done. In all cases the
referenced record, after a possible update, of the xml_id is returned.

- **xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â record xml_id, under the format `module.name`
- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â model name of the record
- **values** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)) â mapping of field names to values the record must fulfill


Examplevalues = {"id": 123}
values = {"name": "INV/2024/0001", "company_id": 1}

the ID of the matched record, `None` if no record found

[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) or [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)

Tip

This function is useful when migrating in-database records into a custom module, to
create the xml_ids before the module is updated and avoid duplication.

Update a record based on its definition in the [Data Files](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/backend/data.html).

This function ignores the `noupdate` flag on the record. It searches in all XML files
from the manifest of the module in the xmlid, or the `from_module` parameter if set,
for a matching definition. When found, it forces the ORM to update the record as in the
specs in the data file.

Optionally this function can reset the translations of some fields.

- **xmlid** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â record xml_id, under the format `module.name`
- **reset_write_metadata** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to update the `write_date` of the record
- **force_create** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether the record is created if it does not exist.
`True` by default, unless `fields` is not None.
- **from_module** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the module from which to update the record, necessary
only when the specs are in a different module than the one in
the xml_id
- **reset_translations** ([*set*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â field names whose translations get reset
- **ensure_references** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether referred records via `ref` XML attributes
should also be updated.
- **fields** ([*set*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#set)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â optional list of fields to include in the XML declaration.
If set, all other fields will be ignored. When set, record
wonât be created if missing.

Warning

This functions uses the ORM, therefore it can only be used after **all** models
referenced in the data specs of the record are already **loaded**. In practice this
means that this function should be used in `post-` or `end-` scripts.

Note

The standard behavior of ORM is to create the record if it doesnât exist, including
its xml_id. That will happen on this function as well, unless `force_create` is set
to `False`.

Remove unused records.

This function will remove records pointed by `xmlids` only if they are not referenced
from any table. For hierarchical records (like product categories), it verifies
if the children marked as cascade removal are also not referenced. In which case
the record and its children are all removed.

Note

The records that cannot be removed are set as `noupdate=True`.

- **xmlids** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â list of xml_ids to check for removal
- **deactivate** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to deactivate records that cannot be removed because
they are referenced, `False` by default
- **keep_xmlids** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to keep the xml_ids of records that cannot be
removed. By default `True` for versions up to 18.0,
`False` from `saas~18.1` on.
- **include_m2m** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*) or *[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â list of m2m tables to include in the search.
`"*"` for all.

list of ids of removed records, if any

[list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)([int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int))

Replace all references to records.

This functions replaces *all* references, direct or indirect to records of `model_src`
by the corresponding records in the mapping. If the target model of the mapping is not
the same as the source one, then `model_dst` parameter must be set.

- **id_mapping** ([*dict*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict)*(*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)*, *[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)*)*) â mapping of IDs to replace, key value is replaced by
the mapped value
- **model_src** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the source model of the records to replace
- **model_dst** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the target model of the records to replace, if `None`
the target is assumed the same as the source
- **replace_xmlid** ([*bool*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)) â whether to replace the references in xml_ids pointing to
the source records
- **ignores** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â list of **table** names to skip when updating the referenced
values

when the target and source model are the same, and the model
table has `parent_path` column, this field will be used to
update it.

Replace values in JSONB columns.

This function replaces `old` by `new` in JSONB values. It is useful for replacing
values in *all* translations of translated fields.

- **table** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â table name where the values are to be replaced
- **column** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â column name where the values are to be replaced
- **old** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â original value to replace, can be a simple term (str) or a Postgres
regular expression wrapped by [`PGRegexp`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.pg.PGRegexp)
- **new** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â new value to set, can be a simple term or a expression using
`<number>` notation to refer to *captured groups* if `old` is a
regexp expression
- **extra_filter** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â extra `WHERE` compatible clause to filter the values to
update, must use the `t` alias for the table, it can also
include `{parallel_filter}` to execute the query in parallel,
see [`explode_execute()`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/upgrades/upgrade_utils.html#odoo.upgrade.util.pg.explode_execute)


### ORMÂ

Utility functions to perform operations via the ORM.

The functions on this module allow to use the ORM safely during upgrades. They enhance or
patch the ORM such that it can handle high volumes of data in a performant way. In some
cases totally different alternatives to the ORMâs own functions are provided. The functions
on this module work along the ORM of *all* supported versions.

Create a new environment.

Warning

This function does *not* empty the cache maintained on the cursor for superuser
with an empty environment. A call to `invalidate_cache` may be necessary every time
data is modified directly in the database.

the new environment

[`Environment`](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/19.0/developer/reference/backend/orm.html#odoo.api.Environment)

Get the names of the models the model directly or indirectly `_inherit`.

**model** â recordset

the names of the models the model directly or indirectly `_inherit`.
If `model._name` is not in the returned set, the model is defined once.
`base` is always in the returned set for non-`base` models.

[list](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)[[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)]

Recompute field values.

This function will recompute fields of a model restricted to a set of records - or
all. The re-computation is not done on all records at the same time. It is split in
batches (chunks). This avoids performance issues, and, in the worse cases, failures
due to `MemoryError`. After each chunk is processed the data is sent back to the
database according to one of the following strategies:

- *flush*: use the `flush` method of the ORM
- *commit*: `commit` the cursor - also flush
- *auto*: pick the best alternative between the two above given the number of records
to compute and the presence of tracked fields.

The *commit* strategy is less prone to cause a `MemoryError` for a huge volume of data.

- **model** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â name of the model to recompute
- **fields** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)*)*) â list of the name of the fields to recompute
- **ids** ([*list*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list)*(*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)*) or *[*None*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None)) â list of the IDs of the records to recompute, when `None`
recompute *all* records, unless `query` is also set (see
below)
- **logger** ([`logging.Logger`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logging.Logger)) â logger used to report the progress
- **chunk_size** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â number of records per chunk - used to split the processing
- **strategy** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â strategy used to process the re-computation
- **query** ([*str*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)) â query to get the IDs of records to recompute, it is an error to set
both `ids` and `query`. Note that the processing will always happen
in ascending order. If that is unwanted, you must use `ids` instead.

Iterate over recordsets.

The `callable` object returned by this class can be used as an iterator that loads
records by chunks (into a `recordset`). After each chunk is exhausted their data is
sent back to the database - flushed or committed - and a new chunk is loaded.

This class allows to run code like:

```python
for record in env['my.model'].browse(ids):
    record.field1 = val

env['my.model'].browse(ids)._compute_field2()
env['my.model'].create(values)

```

in a performant way while also avoiding `MemoryError`, even when `ids` or `values`
have millions of entries. The alternative using this class would be:

Example

```python
MyModel = util.env(cr)['my.model']
for record in util.iter_browse(MyModel, ids):
    record.field1 = val

util.iter_browse(MyModel, ids)._compute_field2()
util.iter_browse(MyModel, ids).create(values)

```

- **model** (`odoo.model.Model`) â the model to iterate
- **ids** (*iterable**(*[*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)*)*) â iterable of IDs of the records to iterate
- **size** ([*int*](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)) â the length of `ids`. Can be used to enable progress logging when `ids` has no `__length__`
